author: joshd4078
content: Because many of you indicated that you already had a pretty good idea of what you are planning to work on, I've started this thread early so that you can signal your project so that others that are interested in the same can start to coordinate.

author: krisoshea.eth
content: Hi there, I’m Kris, delighted to have been selected to participate in cohort 7 of the fellowship! The Reth partial statefulness project really caught my eye early on, ive been following the Reth project for a really long time and this looks like a great opportunity to contribute something meaningful to it. I’ve been researching it for the past few weeks, I’ll share my week 0 update below. So far, it seems like a pretty large research task, I imagine it will take up until the project proposal deadline to really understand the problem deeply and to put together a very well fleshed out project scope. I’m working on my week 1 update at the moment. I’m very excited to work seriously on this project!

author: lqzic
content:
I'm interested in building an in-protocol builder (EIP 7732) on Lodestar, as per their proposed project here:
https://github.com/eth-protocol-fellows/cohort-seven/blob/main/projects/project-ideas.md#lodestar-eip-7732-builder
This caught my eye as I would really like to work on an infrastructure piece that would be used going forward - and I already have some context on it (and Gloas in general) that I got through working on various PRs on Lodestar.
I also like how well-scoped it is.

author: arshlabs
content:
hey, I'm Arsh, participating permissionlessly with my friend <@860371399419887637> 
we are signaling for the Reth Partial Statefulness and State Expiry Prototype.

so yeah we have been working on this since the project list was updated. 
I have 4 merged PRs implementing the EIP 7928 Block-Level Access List types in alloy-rs/eips,  the part of the shared Rust Ethereum library ecosystem that reth and other EL projects further build on. 
EIP-7928 is listed there on the project info as the related work for this project bcause BALs give you a per block record of which accounts & storage slots were accessed which gives the partial state design sort of a dependency info they need, to decide what to keep locally or what can be skipped/fetched.

my week 0 update about it is published you can check it out here:
https://hackmd.io/@arshlabs/HJtE30wWGg

covered the full research stack from MPT fundamentals through Verkle (EIP 6800), state expiry (EIP 7736), VOPS, client storage architectures (Reth, Geth, Erigon) and the existing partial state prototypes (Geth PR #33764, Erigon issue #20587). 
so for the next step, will be diving into the codebases and running nodes as planned!

author: arshlabs
content: also noticed that Kris has also signaled for this project, we have already reached out via DM to discuss for collaboration but haven't heard back yet so yeah we are open to coordinate if there's room, but ofc it makes sense to sort the split work out with Josh and Mario before anyone moves further. looking forward to it!

author: krisoshea.eth
content: Sorry I missed your DM earlier, I replied there but this thread is probably the best place to discuss this now as Josh set it up for this purpose. There seems to be a lot of interest in this project, as far as I can tell around 5-6 people total have signalled interest across the various threads so we’ll probably need some guidance from Mario and Josh in terms of how to move forward and how to scope this

author: krisoshea.eth
content: Also <@1335660981019480075> would you mind sharing some links to your merged PRs implementing EIP7928 block level access list types in alloy-rs/eips? I wanted to see your implementation approach but I can’t seem to find the merged PRs, thanks

author: arshlabs
content: there's a filter by author option in the closed prs right? and for the rest ill get back in a while to discuss about the coordination

author: krisoshea.eth
content: Yep there is but I couldn’t find any related to implementing EIP7928 block level access list types in alloy-rs, can you share them? They’re not coming up under that filter for me

author: krisoshea.eth
content: Oh ye I had a look through those PRs already, but they mainly seem to involve docs changes, adding a helper and adding derives like borsh and hash to existing types. I couldn’t see where BAL types themselves were originally added to Alloy in those PRs. Do you know where I can find the PR where BAL types themselves were added? I’m looking for it at the moment, I’m really interested in seeing the original type design and the type implementation that was merged as well as any discussion that happened around it.

author: krisoshea.eth
content: Ok found the two PRs here

author: arshlabs
content:
just want to clear this up btw. ofc i never said i wrote the original BAL types from scratch, the originals were by Soubhik (previous EPF fellow) and also Ishika
and btw, building on what exists by fixing bugs and adding what's missing is just how open source works!?!?

"mainly docs changes, a helper and derives" doesnt really match wht the diffs show tho do they?
#63 fixes a broken borsh feature flag on Bal where it was the only type not deriving it so yeah the feature silently breaks up..
#81 is across 6 files and the actual decision was intentionally excluding Bal from Hash bcause its correct hash is keccak256 of the RLP encoding not structural equality
#62 fixes wrong type descriptions copy pasted from code_change.rs into nonce_change.rs and adds up a constructor

not sure what your intent for this conversation is tbh!
